event_description: Elections to the 10th European Parliament held across EU member states (6-9 June 2024).
Final day of voting. EPP wins largest share; von der Leyen positioned for second term.

event_name: European Council nominates von der Leyen
event_description: European Council proposes Ursula von der Leyen as Commission President candidate by qualified majority.
10th European Parliament holds constitutive session in Strasbourg. Roberta Metsola re-elected President.

event_name: EP elects von der Leyen as Commission President
event_description: European Parliament elects Ursula von der Leyen for a second term as Commission President (401 votes in favour).
Council, in agreement with President von der Leyen, adopts the list of Commissioners-designate.
President von der Leyen sends mission letters to each Commissioner-designate outlining portfolio responsibilities.
European Parliament committees begin confirmation hearings of Commissioners-designate (4-12 November 2024).
Last round of Commissioner-designate hearings held, including for Ribera and Fitto (controversial EVPs).

event_name: Conference of Presidents endorses full College
event_description: EP Conference of Presidents confirms all Commissioners-designate have passed their hearings.
European Parliament approves the von der Leyen II Commission by roll-call vote: 370 for, 282 against, 36 abstentions.

event_name: Von der Leyen II Commission takes office
event_description: The 2024-2029 European Commission officially takes office.
European Commission adopts the 2025 Commission Work Programme, outlining legislative and policy priorities.
